perturbation.original_text: Subject to the Terms of Section 7 hereof, AGI hereby grants the Company the right to hold the Business out as having the License (the "Endorsement").
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the Terms of Section 7 hereof, AGI hereby grants the Company the right to hold the Business out as having the License (the "Marketing Program").
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Endorsement" to "Marketing Program" creates confusion as the contract refers to both terms. The contract earlier defines 'Endorsement' as the right to hold the business as having the License. By renaming it as 'Marketing Program', it contradicts the original definition and section 5, which separately discusses a 'marketing program' on different terms. This creates uncertainty in enforcement regarding the rights and obligations associated with each concept.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AGI and the Company agree to develop and implement a program for marketing certain products and services offered by AGI through the Good Sam program, including, without limitation, the emergency road service program and the extended warranty program offered through Good Sam. Such marketing program will be on terms acceptable to AGI and the Company and shall be subject to standards and procedures designated by AGI. The terms of the marketing program will be set forth in a marketing agreement between AGI and the Company. The marketing agreement will provide, among other things, the terms pursuant to which the Company shall make "Fulfillment Deposits" for warranty products sold as a part of the marketing program.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Endorsement' to 'Marketing Program' creates a direct contradiction because the contract already defines and discusses a separate 'marketing program' with its own terms and conditions, including 'Fulfillment Deposits.' By renaming the 'Endorsement' (the right to hold the business out as having the License) to 'Marketing Program,' the change creates ambiguity and conflicts about which terms apply to which concept, thereby contradicting the existing section that specifies the terms of the marketing program.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company, the Endorsement may be withdrawn by AGI in its sole discretion and this Agreement terminated pursuant to the terms of Section 3B. In the event of such withdrawal, the Company agrees forthwith to take such action as AGI may request to publicly evidence that the Endorsement has been withdrawn and the Company agrees not to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company, the License may be withdrawn by AGI in its sole discretion and this Agreement terminated pursuant to the terms of Section 3B. In the event of such withdrawal, the Company agrees forthwith to take such action as AGI may request to publicly evidence that the Endorsement has been withdrawn and the Company agrees not to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Endorsement' to 'License' in the first sentence creates a contradiction. The endorsement is the right to *hold* the business as having the license (Section 2), but now the contract states that the *license itself* can be withdrawn due to a breach. This misaligns with the subsequent sentences in the same section, which correctly refer to the withdrawal of the 'Endorsement' which creates in-text contradiction as the start of the phrase reffers to 'License being withdrawn', instead of 'Endorsement'. It introduces ambiguity about whether a breach leads to the loss of the endorsement (right to promote the license) or the license itself (the actual permission to use the Trademarks).
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company, the License may be withdrawn by AGI in its sole discretion and this Agreement terminated pursuant to the terms of Section 3B. In the event of such withdrawal, the Company agrees forthwith to take such action as AGI may request to publicly evidence that the Endorsement has been withdrawn and the Company agrees not to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Endorsement' to 'License' creates a direct contradiction because the sentence initially allowed for the withdrawal of the 'Endorsement' (the right to promote the license), but now states that the 'License' itself can be withdrawn. This contradicts the subsequent sentences in the same section and the contradicted text which refers to the withdrawal of the 'Endorsement', not the license itself. This creates ambiguity regarding whether a breach leads to the loss of endorsement or the license, directly impacting the obligations of the company as defined in the contradicted text, leading to unenforceability (Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction).

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended, modified, superseded, cancelled, renewed or extended, and the terms and conditions hereof may be waived, only by a written instrument signed by the Parties or, in the case of a waiver, by the Party waiving compliance.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended, modified, superseded, cancelled, renewed or extended, and the terms and conditions hereof may be waived, only by a written instrument signed by the Executives or, in the case of a waiver, by the Party waiving compliance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Parties' to 'Executives' introduces a contradiction. The agreement is explicitly between 'Holiday RV Superstores, Inc.' and 'Affinity Group, Inc.' (Section 1), defined as the 'Parties'. Using 'Executives' instead of 'Parties' means that now the written instrument must be signed by the executive personnel instead of the legal entities who are the parties to the agreement (Company and AGI), meaning that the agreement now makes no sense. This directly contradicts the initial agreement setup and creates uncertainty whether the executives are able to make the changes or not, as the document defined them as Parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is made as of the 20th day of March 2002, by and between Holiday RV Superstores, Inc., d/b/a Recreation USA (the "Company"), a Delaware corporation having its principal office at 200 East Broward Boulevard, Suite 920, Ft. Lauderdale, Florida 33301, and Affinity Group, Inc. ("AGI"), a Delaware corporation having its principal office located at 2575 Vista Del Mar Drive, Ventura, California 93001 (each a "Party" and collectively the "Parties"). WHEREAS, the Company is a multi-state chain of dealerships engaged in the retail sales of service of recreational vehicles (the "Business"); and WHEREAS, AGI, either directly or through subsidiaries, operates the "Good Sam" club for recreational vehicle enthusiasts; and WHEREAS, the Company has determined that it would be beneficial sell vehicles with the Good Sam endorsement; and WHEREAS, the Company has raised capital that it represents will be sufficient to fund its operations as projected for at least the next year and AGI is willing to make the Good Sam name available as an endorsement for certain vehicles sold by the Company on the terms and for the consideration set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments to the agreement require a written instrument signed by the 'Parties,' which are explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' as 'Holiday RV Superstores, Inc.' and 'Affinity Group, Inc.'. The changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'Executives,' meaning the amendments would now need to be signed by the executives instead of the legal entities who agreed to the agreement, thus creating a direct contradiction. This alteration introduces uncertainty and conflict regarding who has the authority to modify the agreement, therefore establishing a clear contradiction.
